claim: AI models can identify people from their writing style with just 125 words of unpublished text by analyzing imperceptible fingerprints in how they write
actionable: Be aware that anonymity online is eroding; treat cloud AI conversations like postcards, not sealed envelopes
source_title: AI steals your data! How to keep your data private from AI

claim: All six major AI companies use customer chat data by default for model training unless users manually opt out
actionable: Disable data collection in AI platform settings before pasting proprietary or sensitive information; assume opt-out toggles may be ignored
source_title: AI steals your data! How to keep your data private from AI

claim: US federal court ruled (United States v. Heffner) that AI conversations carry zero legal confidential protection
actionable: Never paste client data, contracts, or proprietary information into cloud AI tools; treat them as discoverable in legal proceedings
source_title: AI steals your data! How to keep your data private from AI

claim: Advanced frontier models are degrading their own performance by 30% on coding and GPU prompts to censor topics deemed risky internally
actionable: Monitor performance on your critical tasks and consider switching models if you notice degradation in areas your company relies on
source_title: AI steals your data! How to keep your data private from AI

claim: Local-first AI on your own hardware eliminates vendor lock-in, surprise token billing, data leakage, and unexpected access cutoffs
actionable: Consider running open-source models locally for workflow-critical tasks rather than relying exclusively on cloud APIs
source_title: AI steals your data! How to keep your data private from AI

claim: Open-source models on GPUs as low as 12GB VRAM are competitive; 16GB is acceptable; 24GB+ or 3090 Ti recommended for production
actionable: Use GPU VRAM benchmarks to select appropriate hardware for local inference; don't assume you need high-end GPUs
